retrospective.harder_than_needed: Two of my own literal design artifacts collided with the very tests meant to verify them, both caught by the delegated dev rather than by me up front: (1) a doc comment I wrote for TICK_MS named the forbidden module filenames (inviteExpirySweep.ts/unreadStore.ts) verbatim, which the VQ-S3-003 structural test bans anywhere in the file including comments; (2) the VQ-S3-005 'no muted reference' check (case-insensitive substring match for "muted") didn't account for the pre-existing, codebase-wide `textMuted` Chakra color token that the component legitimately uses three times for secondary text — a real project-specific gotcha (a design token that substring-collides with an unrelated banned word) worth remembering: when writing a source-text regression test in a no-jsdom repo, grep the intended production content against the test's own regex before treating it as a fixed target, not after.

absorbed_from.note: Fixed two self-inflicted literal-collision bugs in the architect's own handoff: reworded the TICK_MS comment to avoid naming the forbidden module filenames the file's own VQ-S3-003 test bans, and scoped the VQ-S3-005 'no muted' structural test to strip the unrelated `textMuted` Chakra color token (used 3x in the component) before the substring check, so the assertion still fails on a real `muted`/`updateInviteLinkMuted` reintroduction without false-failing on the token.
